per_turn_leaks.student_message: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been having some pretty bad abdominal pain for the past three weeks. It's crampy and mainly feels like it's on my left side. Lately, I've also started getting bloody stools with mucus, and they're really loose and watery. I'm worried about this because it's affecting my work performance and I've never had anything like this before.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, it's not just the pain that's getting to me - it's also the fact that I'm having bowel movements 8-10 times a day and they're so loose, it's hard for me to focus at work. I've tried taking loperamide to slow down my bowels, but it's only given me minimal relief.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I've been feeling pretty fatigued lately, especially after one of those bowel movements. It's like my energy just crashes and I need to take a nap. And yeah, I do get a bit worried about the bleeding - it's bright red blood mixed with stool, which is why I'm here seeing you today.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I've also been experiencing some achy pain in both my knees and ankles, but it's not swelling or redness - just a dull ache that comes and goes. It's not as bad as the abdominal pain, but it's still bothering me because it seems to be getting worse when my stomach is acting up.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I've been experiencing some low-grade fever a few times per week, usually around 37.8-38.0°C. It's not super high, but it's definitely there and can last for a day or two before going away. And as for night sweats, yeah, I do get pretty sweaty during the nighttime episodes when my diarrhea wakes me up from sleep - which is about 2-3 times per week.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've actually been trying to manage my symptoms on my own, but it's not working out too well. As for medications, I'm only using loperamide (Imodium) for the diarrhea, and like I mentioned earlier, it hasn't given me much relief. For the joint pain in my knees and ankles, I've been taking ibuprofen 2-3 times a week to help with the discomfort, but to be honest, that seems to make the abdominal symptoms worse.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My father actually has type 2 diabetes, which is something he's been dealing with for a while now. My mother, on the other hand, seems to be pretty healthy - she doesn't have any major medical conditions that I know of. As for older relatives or siblings, my sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was around 30 years old, but she's doing okay now.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I've had a couple of beers most weeknights before all this started, but over the past month or so, I've actually increased that to 4-5 beers per night because it's been helping me unwind and deal with the stress from my symptoms and work. It's not something I'm proud of, but it's been a coping mechanism for me lately.
